title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam, 28, died in a skydiving accident in Orange, Massachusetts, on Sunday due to a wind gust.
Gaddam, a Northeastern grad and software developer, had recently earned his A License in skydiving.
The incident was attributed to an unpredictable weather phenomenon called a dust devil, according to Jumptown Skydiving.

title: Is the real summer turnaround in Boston sports coming from Legacy FC?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC secured its first road win in history with a 1-0 victory over Orlando Pride.
After a rough 0-5 season start, Legacy FC is making a playoff push with recent wins.
Legacy FC will face second-ranked Washington Spirit at Centreville Bank Stadium on Sunday.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 16, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Nana's Green Tea opens July 17 in Boston's Back Bay, offering matcha drinks and Japanese-inspired desserts.
About Thyme Lebanese Street Café to open August 1 in Harvard Square, serving saj-based wraps and labneh.
C’Yool Yemeni Café and Bakery opens in Boston's South End this July, featuring freshly roasted coffee and Yemeni pastries.

title: 19-year-old dies in apparent drowning in Merrimack River
author: John Waller
bullets: Robert Batchelder, 19, died after struggling while swimming in the Merrimack River near Canterbury, New Hampshire.
Co-workers and bystanders attempted to assist Batchelder, but he submerged before they could reach him.
Concord firefighters retrieved Batchelder, who was later pronounced dead at a local hospital; investigation is ongoing.

title: Pilot rescued after small plane crashes into Plymouth pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A small plane crashed into Curlew Pond in Plymouth, Massachusetts around 8 a.m. Thursday.
The pilot, suffering serious injuries, was rescued by first responders and taken to a nearby hospital.
Authorities are investigating the potential presence of additional occupants on the aircraft.

title: ‘Weird Al’ Yankovic promises to super-size his weirdness at TD Garden Saturday
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: "Weird Al" Yankovic plans a 'Bigger & Weirder' concert at TD Garden on Saturday, July 18.
Yankovic discusses his career longevity, citing a focus on larger productions and steady performance improvements.
The singer has not released a new album since 2014, focusing on tours and a Broadway musical project.

title: The entire Mass. House delegation voted to cut Israel aid. Here’s what they said.
author: The Associated Press and Boston.com Staff
bullets: The entire Massachusetts House delegation voted to cut $3.3 billion in U.S. military aid to Israel.
The vote reflects shifting attitudes within the Democratic Party regarding U.S. support for Israel amidst ongoing conflicts.
Massachusetts representatives cited Israeli military actions and support for Netanyahu's strategies as reasons for their votes.

title: ESPN ranks A.J. Brown as a top-10 wide receiver
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown ranked No. 9 among wide receivers in ESPN's 2026 list, compiled by Jeremy Fowler from NFL insiders.
Brown recorded 1,003 yards and seven touchdowns in 2025, maintaining productivity despite a decrease from previous years.
Brown's performance includes leading the NFL in tight-window catches since 2019, with 88 receptions for 1,812 yards.

title: A ‘once-in-a-lifetime’ weekend on Boston Harbor
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Tall ships from around the world gathered at Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 2026, attracting numerous visitors.
Three New England hotels made Travel + Leisure's list of the world's best, with XV Beacon ranking 55th.
A new waterfront hotel named The Harborvale opened in Burlington, Vermont, on Lake Champlain's shores.

title: 2026-27 Bruins lineup 1.0: Predicting Boston’s lines, D pairs after start to offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Fraser Minten is predicted to have a key role alongside David Pastrnak for the 2026-27 Bruins season.
New defensive pairings include Frederic Brunet with Charlie McAvoy, and Hampus Lindholm with Will Borgen.
Jeremy Swayman is expected to continue as the Bruins' primary goalie, with Michael DiPietro as his backup.
